reviews.review_description: Loved the experience I've always wanted to see this area but there was no radiation sickness or after effects so no 5 stars

developer_response.body: Dear jaco-bagli we can send you a radioactive souvenir from our next expedition as a DLC.

reviews.review_description: I love this app! I am a huge fan of educational stuff. Ty for the heads up on the Bluetooth remote. I figured out my remote was not properly connected. Great customer service to go with the great app. I know it cannot be easy to create apps like this so ty for your hard work! Best Wishes

developer_response.body: Hey Sheerglow, I think a gamepad connected via bluetooth might help you. The controlls for the gamepad are implemented in the application and you can rotate with it.

reviews.review_description: A beautiful trip in the Chernobyl area. Mi of   360 images and cgi is nice, albeit more real section of the reactor area would have been nice. A must see if you are interested in the Chernobyl disaster
It's nice, but after a while the way it's narrated is a bit boring... I got it in a bundle, if not I wouldn't buy it
Great game overall amazing effects
I visited Chernobyl last year, Now I am back there. Thank you very much . Really great app.

Nice environment and idea. Some little bugs and the audio could be better. 
But this is really interesting. Maybe a little expensive for what you get, but it is worth a try.

reviews.review_title: Crashes in about 5 seconds every time
reviews.review_description: I don't know what the problem is, but every time I enter this app it just crashes in 5 seconds, before I can hit the menu...I have tried to launch it from the oculus home or directly from the oculus store library page, but it always crashes. Does this app have some conflict with the xposed framework like the Colab does?

developer_response.body:
The app has been carefully tested with all the compatible devices, however we cannot guarantee the stability on the modified system. Although it is just an assumption but some permissions required by the application might work differently
with some xposed modules.

reviews.review_description: Always wanted to visit Pripyat and Chernobyl. This is the opportunity, I like it a lot! Hope there will come more scenes.

The scenery is beautiful and the narrator is perfect for the setting. This reminds me of a self-guided museum tour. There's a mix of real 360 degree photos and recreated scenes to walk through. I find it really cool. I would like to see more real photos of the place in the future.
As for the $4.99 price point, I'm not so sure. Seems a little bit pricey for an app that can be finished in 30 minutes. In the end, it was a really cool experience though!

Ever since I saw the "Life After People" episode that talked about the Chernobyl disaster on the History Channel, I have wondered what it would be like to tour the ruins of Pripyat. Now I have an alternative opportunity, and I don't need to go to the Ukraine to do it. I love the narrator's voice. I was able to use my SteelSeries Stratus XL gaming controller to turn around. Some of the levels let you walk around, too, while other levels feature 360 degree 3D pictures. Both are fascinating. I really do feel like I was given a tour of a place that time forgot. I wish there would be more apps of more forbidden, dangerous places that we could visit virtually. 

The only thing I can think of that might improve the experience would be more varied, realistic wind sounds for the outside levels and optional subtitles. But other than those two minor suggestions, this app is incredible. I've already spent over an hour altogether visiting each and every level. Thank you so very much.
Cant play,  crash then try open.. (samsung  S6 )

developer_response.body: Could you provide us with more information? What information do you receive after it crashes? When does it occure? Do you use stock android? cvrprsupport@thefarm51.com

reviews.review_description:
Not many people remember Chernobyl’s nuclear disaster, but even less know about the aftermath. For a VR experience, it’s a good beginning. Some of the editing (interviews) is choppy, but it gives a tour to areas that I didn’t know even existed and provides an adequate first-account from survivors. 

I’d love if this would’ve evolved into a docu-VR series, with more content and interviews, but I’m really happy to know that proceeds fro the purchase of Thao product will go to Ukraine.
Really good, obviously it's not sharp on pictures but it's still very good, very informative and Def get ya money's worth, alot of work has gone into this you can see, very pleased I purchased this
Really gutted as it will not install.
Very informative & imaging is great. Me & my family enjoyed this VR experience very much. Hope they add more soon.
Love it thank you xx

Interesting information but the VR experience is lacking.  Minimal interaction and sound is bad.  Most of the environments are static 360 views and the walk-through ones are clunky. I tried using a controller, it worked well until I used an interaction point... then it locked up and would not let me move.  Had to use the visor's pad after that.  Not worth the money in my book.
Very interesting and great visuals l
This was great and is exactly what I was looking for in VR. Not a game,  but interactive. I got to see a place that I would otherwise never see.
